titleStmt.title: SII 25.265: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 265. (A.R. No. 265 of 1908.) SUNĀRPĀL, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: On a slab set up under a tree in the village.
This Sanskrit inscription in Nāgarī characters may be assigned to 12th century on palaeographical grounds.

It does not furnish any date. It records the grant of the village Ādhakāḍa by king Jeyasiṁhadēva of the Nāga race, probably in the presence of his two queens Lōkamahādēvī and the great queen Sāmaṁnadēvī and the five ministers (pañchapradhāna) The two witnesses to this grant are Aṁvāvali-nāyaka and Naḍayā-Brāhmaṇa. The five ministers include the mahāpradhāni Namava-nāyaka, the paḍivāla, chavari-kumara, sarvvavādināyaka (the officer of the intelligence department) and another named Dōdēsari-pātra Chavākā.
This was caused to be written by Pulana-nāyaka.
The term paḍivāla is interesting. It may be synonymous to the expression paḍaivēlan in Tamil meaning an officer of rank in the army.
(Published in Epigraphia Indica; Vol. X, pp. 35-36).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
ṇiki
sura nāgaṁba
vatīpuravareśvara śi (?)
lamārttamḍa savatsavyā
ghralāṁchana kāsyapagotra prakaṭikṛ
ta vijayaghoṣaṇa kaṁjakiṁjalka piṁ
ja Read ‘puñaja’piṁjarita bhramarāyamāṇa rā
jādhirāja mahārāja śrī jeyasiṁ
ghadeve pādā . kāmadevi loka
mahādevi mahādevi sāmaṁnade
vi paṁcapradhāna mahāpradhāni namava
nāyaka dvitīye paḍibālatyatīye
cavari kumaracaturthe sarvvavadi nā
yaka dodesaripātra cavākā Ā
dhakāḍa jesiṁghadevadinhā Aṁvāva
li nāyaku sākṣi naḍayā bāhma
ṇa sākṣidvovu bhaṁḍāri visva
viśya vāri goghanakṛtaghana
ścaiva . brahmahatyā gurutalpaka
Eteṣāṁ sudhina dṛṣṭvā bhūmi harttānasu
dhyati . Aṁnodakeṣvaraṁnaṣu suṣkakoṭa
rasāyuṇaṁ kṛṣṇasarpābhijāyaṁte purvvada
haraṁti je rāmālekapitaṁ pulāmanāyaka .
Digital edition of SII 25.265 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
